channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Hacker obtains House ethics testimony on Matt Gaetz as Trump makes calls for AG nominee
item.description: An unidentified hacker has reportedly obtained a file that contains confidential testimony involving allegations of sexual misconduct by Matt Gaetz.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump has called for dismantling the Education Department. Here's what that would mean
item.description: Throughout his campaign, President-elect Donald Trump heaped scorn on the federal Department of Education, describing it as being infiltrated by “radicals, zealots and Marxists.”.
item.dc:creator: Annie Ma, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's casting call as he builds out his administration: TV experience preferred
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is clearly prizing experience on television as he chooses people to serve in his new administration.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Long, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump Cabinet picks give some of his Muslim voters pause
item.description: Muslims for Trump co-founder Rabiul Chowdhury said some of the nominations are concerning for voters.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about Linda McMahon, Trump’s pick for Education secretary
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Linda McMahon has been a constant presence in Donald Trump's tumultuous orbit, serving in his first administration and supporting his presidential campaigns. Now he's chosen her to serve as Education secretary. Here's a look at McMahon's background, from business to politics.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump picks Dr. Oz to run Medicare and Medicaid, Linda McMahon for Education, Lutnick for Commerce
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday tapped billionaire professional wrestling mogul Linda McMahon to be secretary of the Education Department, tasked with overseeing an agency Trump has promised to dismantle. He also selected Dr.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, AMANDA SEITZ, COLLIN BINKLEY and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nevada attorney general pledges to combat 'unconstitutional mandates' amid Trump election
item.description: Democratic Nevada Attorney General Aaron Ford said his office was prepared to address any “fear and uncertainty” following the election of President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Someone accessed files said to contain damaging info about Trump AG-nominee Gaetz, lawyer says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An unauthorized person gained access to a file containing confidential testimony from women who have made allegations about former Rep. Matt Gaetz, Donald Trump's pick to become the next attorney general, a lawyer said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Few Americans use 'woke' terminology: Survey
item.description: In the YouGov survey, under a quarter of Americans polled said they “regularly use” words and phrases such as “safe space,” “woke,” "lived experience” and “white privilege.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Trump interviewing candidates for FBI chief, Vance says in later-deleted social media post
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump is interviewing candidates for the role of FBI director, incoming Vice President JD Vance said Tuesday in the clearest indication yet that the new administration is looking to replace current director Christopher Wray.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A battle over mail ballots in Pennsylvania is latest example of messy disputes over election rules
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — The recount underway in Pennsylvania's U.S. Senate race marks the end of a chaotic post-election period that has become the latest example of how disputed election rules can expose weak points in a core function of American democracy.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump watches as Musk’s SpaceX launches Starship rocket
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is expected to attend the launch of a SpaceX rocket in Texas on Tuesday, sources familiar with the plans confirmed, in the latest sign of Trump’s closeness to the company's billionaire CEO Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans fume as GOP absences help Democrats move judicial nominees
item.description: Senate Republicans aired frustrations Tuesday after Vice President-elect Vance and other party members skipped votes Monday, greasing the skids for Democratic-backed judicial nominees to be greenlighted as part of a final push to fill the bench with lifetime appointees before President-elect Trump t
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Pro-life groups cautious on RFK Jr. nomination after evolving abortion views
item.description: Pro-life groups are skeptical but optimistic about the nomination of pro-choice Robert F. Kennedy Jr. to lead the Department of Health and Human Services under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Los Angeles passes sanctuary city ordinance in wake of Trump election victory
item.description: Los Angeles voted to become a sanctuary city, as the Trump administration promises to carry out mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump sets records with pace of appointments, but that doesn't mean the transition is going smoothly
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — In the two weeks since Election Day, President-elect Donald Trump has been setting records with the pace of appointments for his incoming administration. But speed shouldn’t be confused with organization.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP senators divided on release of Gaetz ethics report as Trump pressures them to move quickly
item.description: As President-elect Donald Trump digs in on his pick of Matt Gaetz for attorney general, Republican senators are divided over how much information they will demand to move his confirmation — and how much to push back on Trump as he demands that they quickly rubber stamp his Cabinet once he takes offi
item.dc:creator: Mary Clare Jalonick And Stephen Groves, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Manhattan prosecutors ask for additional pause in Trump hush-money criminal case
item.description: Manhattan prosecutors are asking for a further pause in the criminal trial against President-elect Donald Trump in order to give both sides time to weigh the unprecedented nature of the situation.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Trump's hush money case should be paused, not dismissed: Prosecutors
item.description: Trump was convicted in May of falsifying business records to cover up a $130,000 payment his former lawyer Michael Cohen made to porn star Stormy Daniels.
item.dc:creator: Luc Cohen / Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Hacker gains access to testimony in Gaetz investigations: reports
item.description: A hacker gained access to witness testimony related to investigations into former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.), who resigned from Congress last week after President-elect Trump tapped him for attorney general, multiple news outlets reported Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Regina Zilbermints, NewsNation

item.title: Hakeem Jeffries wins reelection as House Democratic leader
item.description: House Leader Hakeem Jeffries won reelection Tuesday as the Democratic leader, receiving support from his colleagues.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Indigenous journalists question the accuracy of poll reported by some media organizations
item.description: An exit poll by Edison Research included a data point that 65 percent of American Indians supported Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Olson, MPR News

item.title: Judge strikes down Wyoming abortion laws, including ban on pills to end pregnancy
item.description: The decision marks another victory for abortion rights advocates after voters in seven states passed measures in support of access.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Top Democrat on House Ethics panel says Gaetz report should be released
item.description: Rep. Susan Wild (D-Pa.), the top Democrat on the House Ethics Committee, said she thinks the panel’s report on former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) should be released.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Rep. Nancy Mace introduces Capitol bathroom bill restrictions
item.description: Mace is currently working with House leadership to include the bathroom restrictions into a rules package.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Trump picks Sean Duffy to be transportation secretary
item.description: Wisconsin Rep. Sean Duffy is a former reality TV star who was one of Trump’s most visible defenders on cable news.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, MICHELLE L. PRICE and DARLENE SUPERVILLE Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: All eyes on House Ethics Committee amid controversy over Gaetz report
item.description: All eyes will be locked on the House Ethics Committee this week as it weighs whether to release its report into Matt Gaetz.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: The last actions the Biden administration will take before Trump takes over the White House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Biden administration officials are working against the clock doling out billions in grants and taking other steps to try to preserve at least some of the outgoing president’s legacy before President-elect Donald Trump takes office in January.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, MATTHEW DALY and COLLIN BINKLEY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NJ Transit, Amtrak testify before New Jersey lawmakers
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (PIX11) - Some palpable tension could be felt during Thursday's Assembly Transportation and Independent Authorities (TIA) Committee, which featured NJ Transit President Kevin Corbett and Amtrak CEO Stephen Gardner.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Trump draft executive order would set up board to oust generals en masse: Report
item.description: If signed by Trump once he takes office, it could allow the quick removal of generals and admirals and purge the ranks of those the future commander-in-chief takes issue with for whatever reason.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, WGN 9
